title: Teaching Delivery System — Priority Tiers, Nightly Base Cut, Verified Spoke Upgrade
what: A two-tier teaching delivery protocol for the WAI-Spoke operational folder. P0/P1 teachings (adopt_asap) are hot-patched directly to spokes on the next session start. P2+ teachings are held in hub staging and never delivered individually — instead, a nightly maintenance job cuts a new base version, collapses all staged teachings into a single consolidated upgrade teaching, and delivers that teaching to spokes. Spokes upgrade by running a verify-apply-verify sequence per constituent teaching, handling partial prior adoption gracefully. Every teaching (individual or consolidated) must carry verification_steps; a teaching without them is rejected at authoring time. Scope is limited to WAI-Spoke/ folder contents — environment configs (CLAUDE.md, hooks, settings.json) remain Basher-managed.
why: Teaching lists on spokes grow unbounded. Agents wade through an ever-deepening pile before each session, adoption is inconsistent, and there is no machine-enforced verification that a teaching was correctly applied. The two-tier model eliminates noise: spokes see only urgent hot-patches and periodic consolidated upgrades. The nightly base cut gives all spokes a clean versioned checkpoint. Verification_steps make adoption auditable and self-correcting — a spoke that partially applied teachings is brought to full compliance automatically by the upgrade sequence.

use_cases.title: P0/P1 teaching authored — hot-patch delivery
use_cases.trigger: Authoring agent writes a teaching with priority P0 or P1 (adopt_asap: true)
use_cases.outcome: Teaching written to hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/teachings/hot-patches/; delivered immediately to all spoke incoming/ queues; applied seamlessly on each spoke's next session start; verification_steps run post-apply; all-pass required to mark accepted

use_cases.title: P2+ teaching authored — staging hold
use_cases.trigger: Authoring agent writes a teaching with priority P2 or lower (adopt_asap: false)
use_cases.outcome: Teaching written to hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/teachings/staging/; NOT delivered to any spoke; held until nightly base cut

use_cases.title: Nightly base cut — consolidation and versioning
use_cases.trigger: Nightly maintenance job runs; at least 1 teaching in staging OR staging count >= 20 (hard cap forces cut)
use_cases.outcome: New base version cut (semver minor bump); consolidated upgrade teaching generated carrying all staged teachings and their verification_steps in sequence; staged teaching files archived to hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/teachings/archive/base-vN/; consolidated upgrade teaching delivered to all spoke incoming/ queues; base version manifest updated

use_cases.title: Spoke applies consolidated upgrade teaching
use_cases.trigger: Spoke Ozi encounters consolidated upgrade teaching in incoming/
use_cases.outcome: For each constituent teaching in the consolidated set: (1) run verification_steps — if all pass, skip (already applied); (2) if any fail, apply the teaching then re-run verification_steps; (3) if all pass after apply, mark accepted; (4) if any still fail, log failure and flag for human review but continue to next teaching. Spoke base_version field updated to match the cut version after all teachings processed.

use_cases.title: New spoke bootstrapped from latest base version
use_cases.trigger: New spoke is initialized (spin-up or fork)
use_cases.outcome: Spoke receives latest base version as its WAI-Spoke/ scaffold — no need to replay historical teachings; base_version set to current; no consolidated upgrade teaching needed since spoke starts at head

patterns.name: Spokes never receive individual P2+ teachings
patterns.description: P2+ teachings exist only in hub staging until the nightly cut. A spoke that checks its incoming/ will never find a raw P2+ teaching — only consolidated upgrade teachings and P0/P1 hot-patches. This is a hard invariant: delivering a P2+ teaching directly to a spoke is a protocol violation.

patterns.name: verification_steps are a required contract, not optional metadata
patterns.description: Every teaching file must have at least one verification_step. A teaching without this field is rejected at schema validation when authored. The verification steps are the machine-enforceable proof that the teaching was applied. Without them, adoption is unverifiable and the upgrade sequence cannot be trusted.

patterns.name: Verify-skip or verify-apply-verify — never blind apply
patterns.description: The upgrade sequence always checks verification_steps first. If they pass, the teaching is skipped. Blind application (apply without prior verification check) risks overwriting state that was correctly set by a different path (e.g., a prior hot-patch). The pre-check is mandatory.

patterns.name: Nightly cut is the only delivery vehicle for P2+ content
patterns.description: The nightly job is not optional infrastructure — it is the single delivery mechanism for non-urgent teachings. Bypassing it (e.g., manually delivering staged teachings to a spoke) breaks version tracking and leaves the base manifest out of sync.

patterns.name: Partial adoption is handled, not assumed
patterns.description: A spoke may have applied some constituent teachings via prior hot-patches or manual action. The consolidated upgrade teaching's verify-first logic detects this and skips already-applied teachings. Partial adoption is a normal state, not an error. The upgrade brings partial adopters to full compliance without re-applying correctly-set state.

patterns.name: Basher-managed items within WAI-Spoke are annotated
patterns.description: Files or directories within WAI-Spoke/ that are owned by Basher (e.g., any Basher-pushed config overlays) carry a managed_by: basher marker (either in a header comment or a .basher-managed sentinel file in the folder). An agent must not modify these directly — changes must be registered with Basher via a lug. This protects Basher-managed state from being clobbered by teaching application.

how.trigger: Teaching authored (P0/P1: immediate; P2+: nightly job). Spoke session start (hot-patch check). Nightly maintenance window (base cut).
steps_summary: 1. Teaching authoring: validate teaching schema — reject if verification_steps missing or empty; set priority + adopt_asap fields; write to hot-patches/ (P0/P1) or staging/ (P2+)

2. P0/P1 hot-patch path: deliver teaching file to all registered spoke incoming/ (via hub-registry.json paths); spoke Ozi picks up on next session start; runs verification_steps post-apply; marks accepted only if all pass
3. P2+ staging: teaching accumulates in hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/teachings/staging/; not visible to spokes

4. Nightly cut (gardener job): if staging count >= 1 — read all staged teachings; generate consolidated upgrade teaching (id: teaching-upgrade-base-vN) carrying each teaching's id, apply_steps, and verification_steps in authoring order; bump base version (semver minor); write consolidated teaching to hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/teachings/consolidated/; move staged files to hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/teachings/archive/base-vN/; update base-version-manifest.json
5. Nightly cut (delivery): deliver consolidated upgrade teaching to all spoke incoming/ queues via hub-registry.json spoke paths

6. Spoke upgrade execution: for each teaching entry in consolidated.teachings[]: (a) run verification_steps — all pass = skip; (b) any fail = run apply_steps then re-run verification_steps; (c) all pass = mark accepted; (d) any still fail = log to WAI-Spoke/teachings/upgrade-failures.jsonl, continue; after all teachings processed: update WAI-Spoke/base_version to consolidated.base_version
7. Hard cap enforcement: if staging count reaches 20 at any point during the day, gardener triggers an out-of-cycle cut immediately rather than waiting for nightly window
constraints: verification_steps field is required on all teachings — authoring is blocked without it
P2+ teachings must not appear in any spoke incoming/ folder directly — only consolidated upgrade teachings are delivered
Spoke base_version is only updated after ALL constituent teachings in a consolidated set have been processed (accepted or flagged)
Hot-patch (P0/P1) teachings bypass staging entirely — they are never consolidated into a base version retroactively
Archive folders are write-once — files in archive/base-vN/ are never modified; they are the historical record of what was consolidated
Basher-managed files within WAI-Spoke/ must not be modified by teaching apply_steps — apply_steps must check for .basher-managed sentinel before writing

teaching_file.description: Schema additions required on all teaching files under this protocol

required_fields.priority: enum: P0 | P1 | P2 | P3 — P0/P1 triggers immediate hot-patch delivery; P2/P3 held for nightly cut
required_fields.adopt_asap: boolean — derived: true if priority is P0 or P1; false otherwise

verification_steps.check: string — the concrete action to take (read file, grep path, inspect field value, etc.)
verification_steps.pass_criteria: string — the condition that must be true for this step to pass

apply_steps.action: string — the concrete action to take to apply this teaching element

consolidated_upgrade_teaching.description: Generated by the nightly cut job; delivered to spoke incoming/ in place of individual P2+ teachings

fields.base_version: string — semver of the new base version this upgrade reaches
consolidates: array of teaching ids subsumed into this upgrade

base_version_manifest.description: Tracks current base version and cut history; lives at hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/teachings/base-version-manifest.json

cuts.archive_path: string — relative path to archive folder for this version

spoke_paths.base_version: WAI-Spoke/base_version — single-line file containing current base semver
spoke_paths.upgrade_failures: WAI-Spoke/teachings/upgrade-failures.jsonl — log of teaching verification failures after apply
spoke_paths.basher_sentinel: WAI-Spoke/<folder>/.basher-managed — presence means folder is Basher-owned; teaching apply_steps must not write here

tests.description: A teaching file without verification_steps field or with an empty verification_steps array is rejected at authoring time
tests.pass_criteria: Schema validation raises error on teaching with missing or empty verification_steps; file is not written to staging/ or hot-patches/

tests.description: A teaching with priority P0 or P1 is written to hot-patches/ and to spoke incoming/, not to staging/
tests.pass_criteria: After authoring a P0 teaching: hot-patches/ contains the file; staging/ does not contain it; at least one spoke incoming/ contains it

tests.description: A teaching with priority P2 or P3 lands in staging/ and does not appear in any spoke incoming/
tests.pass_criteria: After authoring a P2 teaching: staging/ contains the file; no spoke incoming/ folder contains it; hot-patches/ does not contain it

tests.description: Nightly cut with N staged teachings produces one consolidated upgrade teaching with type=consolidated and consolidates[] listing all N teaching ids
tests.pass_criteria: consolidated/ contains teaching-upgrade-base-vN.json; file has type=consolidated; consolidates array length == N; all original teaching ids present in consolidates[]

tests.description: After nightly cut, staged teachings are moved to archive/base-vN/ and staging/ is empty
tests.pass_criteria: staging/ is empty after cut; archive/base-vN/ contains all N original teaching files; base-version-manifest.json updated with new version entry

tests.description: A spoke that already satisfies a teaching's verification_steps sees that teaching skipped (not re-applied) during upgrade
tests.pass_criteria: Apply consolidated upgrade to a spoke where teaching T1 was already applied; upgrade log shows T1 skipped; T1 apply_steps not executed; upgrade-failures.jsonl has no entry for T1

tests.description: A spoke that fails verification for a teaching has that teaching applied and then re-verified
tests.pass_criteria: Apply consolidated upgrade to a spoke missing teaching T2; upgrade log shows T2 apply_steps executed; verification_steps re-run post-apply; all pass; T2 marked accepted; upgrade-failures.jsonl has no entry for T2

tests.description: A teaching whose verification_steps still fail after apply is logged in upgrade-failures.jsonl and the upgrade continues to the next teaching
tests.pass_criteria: Simulating a teaching whose apply_steps cannot satisfy verification_steps: upgrade-failures.jsonl contains an entry with the teaching id and failure details; subsequent teachings in the consolidated set are still processed

tests.description: After a spoke successfully processes a consolidated upgrade teaching, WAI-Spoke/base_version matches the consolidated teaching's base_version field
tests.pass_criteria: cat WAI-Spoke/base_version == consolidated_teaching.base_version after upgrade completes

tests.description: When staging count reaches 20, a cut is triggered immediately without waiting for the nightly window
tests.pass_criteria: Insert 20th teaching into staging/; nightly job or cap-watcher triggers cut; consolidated teaching generated; staging cleared; cut timestamp in manifest is not the scheduled nightly time

health.verification_note: Tools built and tested S42: validate_teaching.py (17 tests), teaching_delivery_init.py (idempotent, run against hub), teaching_delivery_cut.py (13 tests), teaching_upgrade_apply.py (10 tests). 40/40 passing. Impl lugs open, awaiting dispatch.

perceive: Read this spec (spec-teaching-delivery-system-v1). Read related spec spec-harness-self-maintaining-wheel-v1 for base harness versioning context. Check hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/teachings/ for current folder structure. Check existing teaching files in hub teaching_repo for current schema (identify if priority and verification_steps fields exist). Check WAI-SpecIndex.jsonl for any overlapping specs.
execute: Implement in four phases: (1) Teaching schema enforcement — add priority, adopt_asap, verification_steps, apply_steps as required fields to teaching schema validator; reject files missing these at authoring time. (2) Hub teaching folder structure — create staging/, hot-patches/, consolidated/, archive/ directories under hub/WAI-Spoke/hub/teachings/; create base-version-manifest.json at initial version. (3) Nightly cut job — implement or extend existing maintenance script to read staging/, generate consolidated upgrade teaching, archive staged files, update manifest, deliver consolidated teaching to spokes via hub-registry.json paths. (4) Spoke upgrade execution — implement verify-apply-verify loop in spoke Ozi session start handler; write upgrade-failures.jsonl on persistent failure; update WAI-Spoke/base_version on completion. Hard cap (20 teachings) check can be added to the gardener's pre-cut trigger. Basher-managed sentinel check (.basher-managed file) must be added to apply_steps executor.
verify: All 10 tests in tests[] pass. Teaching file without verification_steps is rejected. P0 teaching appears in spoke incoming/ without going through staging. P2 teaching accumulates in staging and is not visible to spokes. Nightly cut produces consolidated teaching with correct consolidates[] array. Spoke upgrade correctly skips already-applied teachings and applies missing ones. upgrade-failures.jsonl written on persistent failure. WAI-Spoke/base_version updated after upgrade.
